clinical_history: The ECG from an elderly man shows which ONE of the following mechanisms?
diagnosis: This ECG shows classic atrial fibrillation (AF) pattern with a rapid ventricular response of about 150/min and baseline artifact. Left axis deviation consistent with left anterior fascicular block is present. Slow R wave progression, a delayed precordial transition zone is present, along with non-specific ST-T changes.  There are borderline voltage criteria (Cornell: S in V3 + R in aVL > 28 mm in men); S in V3 + R in aVL > 20 mm in women) for left ventricular hypertrophy in this patient with documented coronary artery disease and long-standing hypertension.
